registered_lanes.notes: registered but no docs/LAW_CLA_LLM/LANE_02 folder found
NTS-owned Uniton OS build lane; scaffold present

topology_mismatches: Root README describes docs/00_ECOSYSTEM_CANON and docs/01_AIER_COMMON as main tiers, while active Lane law system is docs/LAW_CLA_LLM/SHARED.
LAW_SYSTEM uses shorthand SHARED/ and LANE_<N>/ paths; real filesystem nests them under docs/LAW_CLA_LLM/.
SHARED_INDEX declares os_operations/OS_OPERATIONS_GUIDE.md, but the discovered file is os_operations/README.md plus skeleton procedure files.
SHARED/VERSION is v1.1.0 while root VERSION is v1.0.0.
Lane_02 is registered, but no docs/LAW_CLA_LLM/LANE_02 folder exists in the current tree.
Lane_01 is represented by legacy docs/LAW_CLA_LLM/CLA_01_VULTR rather than a normalized LANE_01 folder.

ready: Tier 1 ecosystem canon docs exist under docs/00_ECOSYSTEM_CANON.
SHARED law system exists under docs/LAW_CLA_LLM/SHARED/laws.
Lane_03 has minimum boot, index, repo registry, conventions, tech, infrastructure, projects, and skills files.
Task evidence pattern exists through reports, snapshots, and audit_logs.
Basic skill and adapter source scaffolds exist.
partial: Adapters exist for claude-web, claude-code, cursor, and codex stub; Codex adapter is not implemented.
Skills exist at root, but SHARED skills are skeleton and skill law references use older LAW naming.
OS operations procedures are mostly skeleton.
CI validates older docs/00_ECOSYSTEM_CANON and skill schemas, not the newer SHARED/LANE topology.
Prompts are mostly legacy Stream/CLA/CLAC oriented.
missing: No Uniton OS repo baseline architecture document for Lane_03 build work.
No module registry for Uniton OS runtime modules.
No runtime adapter contracts in contracts/.
No skill contract schema tying skills to SHARED laws and Lane load order.
No Lane_03 project context for an actual Uniton OS repo.
No CI guard enforcing docs/LAW_CLA_LLM/SHARED and LANE_<N> rules.
unclear: Whether older AIER_COMMON laws remain binding beside the new SHARED/Lane system.
Whether root VERSION or SHARED/VERSION is authoritative for release gating.
How releases should reconcile generated dist artifacts with [vercel skip] governance commits.
Whether Lane_01 and Lane_02 should be normalized into LANE_01 and LANE_02 folders.
risks: Index drift between README, SHARED_INDEX, root VERSION, SHARED/VERSION, and actual filesystem.
Build workflow on main uses commit message [skip ci], not the required [vercel skip] suffix.
Contracts and handoffs are empty, so cross-Lane/runtime integration is not yet governed by concrete artifacts.
Codex path is stubbed while Lane_03 default executor includes Codex-capable execution.
